subject: Physical Education

sections.heading: ⚔️ The Quest Begins — What is Kho-Kho?
sections.body: Welcome, young hero! Kho-Kho is one of India's most exciting traditional sports — like a quest where one team chases and the other team escapes! Two teams battle it out. The chasing team tries to tap players from the other team, while the defending team uses speed and tricks to escape. Think of it like a super-fast game of 'pakad-pakad' that you might play in your colony, but with special rules and skills!
keyPoints: Kho-Kho needs speed, agility, quick thinking, leg strength and teamwork — just like a real quest!
One team chases and taps, the other team dodges and escapes — then they swap roles.
The Kho-Kho court for sub-junior players is 14 × 24 square metres — about the size of a classroom doubled!

sections.heading: 🧙 Mastering the Sitting Stance and Chasing Spell
sections.body: Every wizard needs a power pose — in Kho-Kho, it's called the Sitting Stance! You sit on your toes inside a small block drawn on the ground, place your hands down like a bridge, and lean slightly forward. This is your ready position before you spring into action like a tiger! When the chaser gets up to run, they step their left leg forward to go left, and right leg forward to go right — just like choosing which side to dribble in cricket fielding!
keyPoints: The sitting stance balances your body weight on both toes and hands — building leg and arm strength.
When chasing, keep your head up, swing arms naturally and always keep your eyes on the defender — like a hawk watching its target!
A fun drill: 20 seconds sitting stance, then frog jumps and duck walk — a mini obstacle quest!

sections.heading: 🐉 The Kho Signal — Passing the Power to Your Teammate
sections.body: In Kho-Kho, you cannot chase forever alone — you pass the power to your teammate using the 'Kho' signal! You tap your teammate on the back AND shout 'Kho' at the same time. It's like passing the baton in a relay race during your school Sports Day. There are three types of Kho — Simple touch Kho, Early Kho and Late Kho. The trick is timing it perfectly so your teammate is ready to sprint!
keyPoints: You must tap the back AND shout 'Kho' together — both signals must happen at the same time.
At least one of your legs must be behind the cross lane when you give the Kho — this is a key rule!
When receiving the Kho, both hands of the chaser must be placed outside the centre line.

sections.heading: 🌀 Dodging and Zig-Zag — The Defender's Magic Powers
sections.body: Defenders in Kho-Kho are like magical tricksters who confuse the chasers! The secret weapon is dodging — you lean your body one way but actually run the other way, just like a batsman faking a shot to confuse the fielder! You can also do quick turns and change your speed suddenly — run slow, then ZOOM fast — to catch chasers off guard. Zig-zag running, both forward and sideways, makes you super hard to catch!
keyPoints: Leaning in one direction and moving in another is called 'wrong footing' — it confuses the chaser completely!
Quick turns and speed changes are your best tricks as a defender.
Zig-zag running has two types — straight zig-zag and sidewards zig-zag — both make you agile like a mongoose!

sections.heading: 🏆 Pole Turn and Covering Chase — Quest for Victory!
sections.body: The Pole Turn is a superpower for chasers — it helps them spin around the pole at the ends of the court and keep chasing without losing speed. You plant your inner foot near the pole, pivot your body, swing your free leg around and push off — like spinning around a lamppost in your gully! Covering Chase is the ultimate team strategy where chasers work together, reading the defender's moves and choosing the perfect moment to attack — just like fielders in cricket setting a trap for the batsman!
keyPoints: In a Pole Turn, plant your inner foot firmly and use a pivot motion — keeping elbows flexed gives you better control and speed.
Covering Chase means the chaser walks smartly along the cross line, watches the defender, and attacks at exactly the right moment.
Working as a team and anticipating your teammate's next move is what makes a champion Kho-Kho player!

summary: Young hero, you have completed the Kho-Kho Quest! In this exciting unit, you discovered the fundamental skills of India's amazing traditional sport — Kho-Kho. You learned the powerful Sitting Stance that builds your strength and keeps you ready to spring into action. You mastered the art of Chasing with the right footwork, and learned how to pass power to your teammate using the magical 'Kho' signal. You unlocked the defender's tricks of Dodging, leaning, wrong footing and Zig-Zag running to escape chasers. Finally, you conquered the Pole Turn technique and the smart team strategy of Covering Chase. Kho-Kho is not just a game — it is a quest that builds your speed, agility, teamwork and quick thinking. Keep practising, keep playing and may you become a true Kho-Kho wizard!
